hook: Luca stands in his flat the morning after the break-in, holding the note with E. Ward's name — the only lead the thief did not take.
goal: Find out who E. Ward is and make face-to-face contact with them.

start_state.time: morning, the day after the metal box was stolen
knowledge: The metal box has been stolen from his flat
Ray Brennan is the most likely thief
The Cole family left Harden in July 1974 without warning
E. Ward was listed as a witness on the 1974 Cole House property record
suspicions: E. Ward may know why the Cole family left Harden
E. Ward may have a personal connection to Ray Brennan

continuity_rules: Must start after last_scene_position — Luca already knows the box is gone; do not re-show that discovery
Must not repeat the library newspaper-and-property-record scene from Episode 3 — this library visit has a single, different purpose: identify E. Ward
Must not contradict established facts: Cole family left July 1974; E. Ward witnessed the property sale; Ray Brennan has the stolen box
key_events: Luca examines the note in his flat and decides that E. Ward is now his only lead; despite the break-in being a clear warning, his curiosity overrides his fear and he leaves for the library.

At the library the librarian checks the town register and tells him Edward Ward died two years ago — BUT his widow, Agnes Ward, still lives on Birch Street; she writes the address on a slip of paper and hands it to Luca.

Therefore Luca walks to Birch Street and knocks on a small, neat house; an elderly woman with white hair opens the door; she is calm until Luca says he is working at the Cole House, and her whole expression changes — she grips the door frame and goes nervous.

Agnes denies knowing anything about the Cole House and begins to close the door; but through the narrowing gap Luca sees a framed photograph on the hallway wall — three men standing together outside the Cole House — before the door clicks shut.
major_reveal: E. Ward is Edward Ward, now deceased; his widow Agnes Ward lives in Harden, and a photograph inside her home places her late husband directly at the Cole House — confirming the Ward family had a personal connection to the Cole family that Agnes is actively hiding.
threads_advanced: thread_005 — E. Ward is now identified and located; Agnes Ward's fearful reaction and her denial confirm she is protecting a secret connected to the Coles and to that house
tone_guardrails: Agnes Ward must not confess or explain — she only reacts physically; the revelation comes from the photograph alone, not from her words
Exactly one major revelation: the Ward-Cole House connection established by the photograph
The scene at the door must be brief and grounded — no speeches, no dramatic confrontation; just a closed door and one glimpsed image
forbidden_moves: Do not reveal what is inside the metal box — that is reserved for Episode 5
Do not have Agnes Ward mention or confirm Ray Brennan's involvement — that is Episode 5
Do not re-show or re-describe the stolen-box discovery from Episode 3
Do not introduce any new unanswered question beyond the one ending hook
ending_hook: The door is closed and Agnes Ward has said almost nothing — but Luca saw the photograph: three men standing outside the Cole House, one of them her husband. She knows exactly what happened fifty years ago. Will she open the door again?
